data.title: Hick's Law
data.description: Adobe's interfaces are designed to reduce decision fatigue by minimizing complexity.
data.technical_spec: Consistent use of minimalistic UI components.

data.title: Fitts's Law
data.description: Interactive elements are optimally sized and positioned to facilitate quick access.
data.technical_spec: Larger buttons in Adobe interfaces for increased accessibility.

data.title: Miller's Law
data.description: Grouping of features into manageable chunks to prevent cognitive overload.
data.technical_spec: UI design leveraging card-based layout systems.

data.title: Law of Proximity
data.description: Elements that are related are placed close to each other to enhance user comprehension.
data.technical_spec: Grid systems used for precise alignment of UI elements.

data.title: Aesthetic-Usability Effect
data.description: Highly aesthetic interfaces enhance perception of usability.
data.technical_spec: Use of Adobe's creative cloud suite for design consistency.
